job_title: Software Engineer III, Performance, AI and Infrastructure
job_nlp_title: Software Engineer III, Performance, AI and Infrastructure
taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, General Software Engineer
core_skills: C++, Python, Performance Analysis, Large Scale Systems, Data Analysis
skill_match_scores: C++:0.0, Python:1.0, Performance Analysis:1.0, Large Scale Systems:0.0, Data Analysis:1.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience, 2 years of experience programming in C++ or Python, 2 years of experience with performance, large scale systems data analysis, visualization tools, or debugging
preferred_quals: Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science or related technical fields, 2 years of experience with data structures or algorithms, Experience developing accessible technologies, Proficiency in code and system health, diagnosis and resolution, and software test engineering
core_responsibilities: Write product or system development code, Participate in, or lead design reviews with peers and stakeholders to decide amongst available technologies, Review code developed by other developers and provide feedback to ensure best practices (e.g., style guidelines, checking code in, accuracy, testability, and efficiency), Contribute to existing documentation or educational content and adapt content based on product/program updates and user feedback, Triage product or system issues and debug/track/resolve by analyzing the sources of issues and the impact on hardware, network, or service operations and quality
job_summary: Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. In this role, you will design, develop, test, deploy, maintain, and enhance software solutions critical to Google’s needs, while managing project priorities and deliverables.
